marketState: neutral-to-bearish - oversupplied storage and a broad bearish technical trend are offset by hot near-term weather demand and oversold conditions
summary: Price ($2.94) sits almost exactly on fair value across every horizon out to +21D ($2.42-$3.52 band), so there is no clean mispricing to trade off. The bearish case is fundamental: storage is +184 Bcf above the 5y average with a +72 Bcf bearish build surprise, seasonality shows price already -10.3pp below its typical-year path with a -3.1% typical 4-week move and only 31-38% odds of being higher in 1-4 weeks (though reliability is flagged Low, a 50pp spread). Technicals confirm the downtrend structurally (50<200 EMA on every timeframe from 15m to 1w) but 4h RSI (24.9) and the 1d close below its lower Bollinger band flag a stretched, oversold short-term condition. Weather is the one genuinely bullish input right now - demand is +14.6% vs 5y in the next 7 days (rangePos 0.86) easing to +7% in days 8-16 - which argues for near-term price support even as the medium-term trend stays soft. COT shows managed money net short (-60,295, 35th percentile) but not at an extreme, so there's room for the short trade to build without an obvious squeeze risk yet.

longTerm.entry: $2.95 - $3.05 (scale in on any bounce toward fair value)
longTerm.rationale: Storage is well above the 5-year average with a bearish surprise build, seasonality shows price already trailing its typical-year path with a negative typical 4-week move, and every technical timeframe from 15m to 1w is in a bearish 50<200 EMA structure. COT managed money is net short but only at the 35th percentile, leaving room to add short exposure without fighting a crowded positioning extreme.
longTerm.invalidation: A sustained storage deficit vs 5y, price reclaiming above $3.15-3.20 (above the Nov26 curve level) with EMA structure flipping bullish, or seasonality probHigher readings rising well above 50%.

shortTerm.rationale: 4h RSI is deeply oversold (24.9) and the 1d close sits below its lower Bollinger band, both consistent with a near-term mean-reversion bounce, while weather demand is running +14.6% above 5-year norms with rangePos near the top of its range (0.86), giving fundamental support to a bounce. This is a tactical countertrend trade against the still-bearish daily/weekly trend, so conviction is low and sizing should stay small.
shortTerm.invalidation: A break and close below $2.78, or demand_1_7D pctVs5y rolling over toward/below the 5y norm, would remove the fundamental support for the bounce.
